steps.title: Trace the poem's contrast
steps.content: The poem begins with renewal: new roads, a new drumbeat, and fresh seeing. The turn comes with But, after which the poem says the same backs will bend and the same sad feet will drum. That structure makes repeated human life both newly hopeful and persistently difficult.

steps.title: Reject narrower purposes
steps.content: Choice B invents a comparison about memorability at different times of day. Choice C turns the poem into a claim about whether joy is more common than sadness. Choice D adds an individual-versus-community contrast the poem never makes. The purpose is to consider repeated life as both rewarding and challenging. The answer is A.
